claim: LMArena raised $100M in Series A at $1.7B valuation with $30M annualized consumption revenue ($2.5M MRR) after launching evals product
actionable: Understand that eval platforms can be monetized through metered consumption model rather than paid leaderboard access, scaling with user adoption
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: LMArena has 250+ million conversations with 5-6 million users monthly, with 25% being software professionals - one of largest LLM consumer platforms outside ChatGPT
actionable: Use organic user voting and diverse user base (including domain experts) to establish leaderboard legitimacy based on real-world usage patterns
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: Public leaderboard integrity is non-negotiable - no paid placement, no scoring manipulation, transparent about pre-release testing with community consent
actionable: When building eval platforms, maintain strict integrity controls and transparency about methodology to establish trust as the canonical benchmark
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: LMArena's response to 'leaderboard illusion' paper showed factual errors - only 60/40 open vs closed source sampling (not 9/60 claimed), pre-release testing always disclosed
actionable: Critically evaluate benchmark critique papers for methodological rigor and factual accuracy before accepting claims about eval bias
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: Nano Banana (Google Gemini 2 Flash) became a cultural sensation on LMArena and drove billions in stock impact - demonstrates cultural/market power of eval moments
actionable: Understand that dramatic leaderboard breakthroughs can signal genuine capability shifts; use eval platforms to identify these market-moving moments early
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: Multimodal models (especially image generation) are becoming economically critical for enterprise - fastest-growing adoption in marketing and design
actionable: Invest in multimodal eval infrastructure for marketing/design use cases as this segment is driving significant ROI in enterprise AI adoption
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: Persistent login history (allowing users to track their own performance over time) was a major driver of user retention on LMArena
actionable: For consumer AI products, implement user history and progress tracking features as core retention mechanisms - simple but highly effective
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena

claim: Code Arena focuses on agent evaluation more than model evaluation - should evolve to test full-featured harnesses like Devon rather than just base models
actionable: When evaluating agents, test the complete system (model + prompting + tools) rather than just the model in isolation for realistic capability assessment
source_title: [State of Evals] LMArena's $1.7B Vision — Anastasios Angelopoulos, LMArena
